title: News Corp Stock Down 25% In Six Months, What's Next?
text: After a 25% decline over the last six months, at the current price of around $16 per share, we believe News Corp stock (NASDAQ: NWSA), a global, diversified media and information services company, could see gains.

title: News Corp's Dow Jones to Participate in the Credit Suisse 24th Annual Communications Conference
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--News Corp announced today that Chief Executive Officer of Dow Jones and Publisher of The Wall Street Journal Almar Latour will participate in the Credit Suisse 24th Annual Communications Conference on Wednesday, June 15, 2022. The virtual session will begin at 9:30am ET. To listen to the live webcast, please visit the News Corp website at https://investors.newscorp.com/calendar-events. title: REA Group Hosts First Investor Day
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--REA Group is targeting "double-digit revenue and EBITDA growth through the cycle,” as it foresees "significant growth opportunities" in its core business and adjacencies (REA defines the property cycle as 3-5 years).1 REA is Australia's largest digital real estate business and is 61.4% owned by News Corp; the company shared the update at its first Investor Day in Sydney, Australia earlier today. Highlights from the REA Investor Day included: REA target of double-digit

title: News Corp Completes Acquisition of Base Chemicals
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--News Corp announced that it has completed the acquisition of the Base Chemicals business from S&P Global Market Intelligence. The business will operate under the name Chemical Market Analytics by OPIS, a Dow Jones company. News Corp acquired OPIS (the Oil Price Information Service) and related assets from S&P Global and IHS Markit in February.
